title: Weight loss: ghrelin up, leptin and insulin down
vignette: Fifty obese adults without diabetes mellitus are enrolled in a 10-week weight loss program. The program includes a low-calorie diet and moderate-intensity exercise for at least 150 minutes per week. The study subjects who are adherent to the regimen lose an average of 7 kg (15.5 lb) at the end of 10 weeks. Venous blood samples to study biochemical changes induced by weight loss are taken at the start of the trial and at the 10-week follow-up visit. Which of the following hormonal changes are likely to occur during the course of the study?

clues.note: Baseline obesity means leptin and insulin start HIGH (and partly resistant), while ghrelin is relatively suppressed. The question is about the DIRECTION these shift with weight loss, so the obese starting point frames the change.
Caloric restriction is the driver. A negative energy balance is the physiologic signal that moves all three appetite hormones - it triggers the body's 'regain' defenses.

Falling fat mass and a calorie deficit raise GHRELIN (hunger hormone, from the stomach) and lower LEPTIN (tracks fat stores) and INSULIN (improved sensitivity, less secretion). This pro-appetite shift is why lost weight is hard to keep off.

whatMatters: Weight loss/caloric restriction shifts appetite hormones toward EATING MORE: ghrelin (hunger, gastric) rises, while leptin (adiposity signal) and insulin (both satiety signals to the hypothalamus) fall. Answer A - ghrelin up, leptin down, insulin down.
strategy: Anchor each hormone to its source/signal: ghrelin = stomach, fasting, hunger (UP with weight loss); leptin = adipocytes, tracks fat mass (DOWN as fat falls); insulin = beta cells, tracks intake/adiposity (DOWN with restriction and better sensitivity). Any answer row that doesn't read up/down/down is wrong. Reverse the arrows for weight GAIN.

eliminations.label: Ghrelin down, leptin down, insulin down - ghrelin wrong
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: leptin and insulin do fall with weight loss, so this row is half right. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: ghrelin RISES with caloric restriction (it is the fasting/hunger hormone) - a fall would be expected after sleeve gastrectomy (which removes ghrelin-producing fundus), not diet + exercise. ✅ Correct if: weight loss achieved by sleeve gastrectomy (ghrelin drops).

eliminations.label: Ghrelin down, leptin up, insulin up - this is weight GAIN
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: a satiated, fed, fat-gaining state. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: it is the exact opposite of caloric restriction - overfeeding suppresses ghrelin and raises leptin and insulin. ✅ Correct if: the subjects had GAINED weight/overeaten.

eliminations.label: Ghrelin up, leptin down, insulin up - insulin wrong
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: correct on ghrelin and leptin. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: insulin DECREASES with weight loss as insulin sensitivity improves and secretion falls - it does not rise. ✅ Correct if: a setting of rising glucose load/insulin resistance, not active weight loss.

eliminations.label: Ghrelin up, leptin up, insulin down - leptin wrong
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: correct on ghrelin and insulin. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: leptin is secreted in proportion to fat mass, so losing fat LOWERS leptin - it does not rise. ✅ Correct if: fat mass were increasing.

reasoning: Caloric restriction creates a negative energy balance
Ghrelin (stomach, hunger) rises to drive appetite and defend weight
Leptin (adipocyte signal) falls as fat mass drops; insulin falls as sensitivity improves
-> ghrelin up, leptin down, insulin down; answer A
mechanism: Three hormones regulate energy balance. Ghrelin is secreted by the stomach during fasting, acts on the hypothalamus to STIMULATE appetite, and rises with caloric restriction. Leptin is released by adipocytes in proportion to fat stores and SUPPRESSES appetite, so it falls as fat mass declines; insulin similarly acts centrally to reduce appetite and decreases with weight loss as insulin sensitivity improves. The net effect of dieting - higher ghrelin with lower leptin and insulin - increases hunger and is a major reason lost weight tends to be regained.

difficulty.label: 📚 Pure knowledge gap
difficulty.message: First Aid Physio/Endo > appetite hormones: ghrelin (stomach, fasting, hunger), leptin (adipocytes, satiety, tracks fat), insulin (satiety centrally); weight loss -> ghrelin up, leptin & insulin down.

difficulty.label: 🧭 Understanding the question
difficulty.message: The stem asks the DIRECTION of change with weight loss. Lock ghrelin = hunger (up) and leptin/insulin = satiety (down), then find the matching row.

difficulty.label: 🔤 Vocabulary
difficulty.message: Unsure on ghrelin, leptin, energy homeostasis, insulin sensitivity, or 'preformed' vs secreted hormones? Tell me which.

difficulty.label: 🧲 Pulled by a distractor
difficulty.message: Row B (all three down) tempts because leptin and insulin do fall - but ghrelin RISES with dieting; it only falls after sleeve gastrectomy.

difficulty.label: ✍️ Something else
difficulty.message: Bank: weight LOSS = ghrelin up / leptin down / insulin down; weight GAIN = the reverse; sleeve gastrectomy lowers ghrelin specifically.

keywords.back: Stomach; fasting; STIMULATES appetite; rises with weight loss
Adipocytes; tracks fat mass; SUPPRESSES appetite; falls with weight loss
Suppresses appetite; falls with weight loss/improved sensitivity
Ghrelin up, leptin down, insulin down (pro-appetite, defends weight)

firstAid.detail: Physio > ghrelin (hunger, up with fasting/weight loss); leptin & insulin (satiety, down with weight loss).
firstAid.location: Physio > ghrelin (hunger, up with fasting/weight loss); leptin & insulin (satiety, down with weight loss).

firstAid.detail: Endo > leptin proportional to fat stores; obesity = leptin resistance; weight loss lowers leptin and triggers regain.
firstAid.location: Endo > leptin proportional to fat stores; obesity = leptin resistance; weight loss lowers leptin and triggers regain.

firstAid.detail: GI/Endo > sleeve gastrectomy reduces ghrelin; weight loss otherwise raises ghrelin.
firstAid.location: GI/Endo > sleeve gastrectomy reduces ghrelin; weight loss otherwise raises ghrelin.

memoryHook: GHRelin = GRRR, I'm hungry. Lose weight and the stomach growls louder (ghrelin up) while the fat-and-fed signals quiet down (leptin & insulin down).
